name: dm
review: in "The Playce" basement

name: Bikini Cabanyal
address: C/ de les Drassanes, 52, Poblats Marítims, 46011 València, Valencia

name: Alexa
review: at the food court

name: Ikea Lichtenberg
review:
Ikea is ideal for a visit with a Baby.
They have everything you need and even a room for private nursing.

name: East Side Mall
review: There is a dm downstairs, but if you  are upstairs, want some privacy and maybe sit down to feed, thats a great option and it's squeeky clean, large and in good condition :)

name: RIGATONI Familien- und Nachbarschaftstreff
review: Very nice, no defects at all! Already changed my baby many times there :)

name: Benedict Prenzlauerberg
review: Clean

name: Westfield Hamburg-Überseequartier
review: There is a kids bathroom and a microwave in the dedicated area.

name: K-Fetisch
review: It is a clapping table.

name: Plansche im Plänterwald (Wasserspielplatz)
review: Very nice and clean for a Plansche.

name: Fiore
review: not often used

name: Pomeranze
review: They don't have a real table but a cover that goes on top of the toilet.

name: Humboldt Forum
review: Amazing! They have many changing tables in all museum areas available. Excelent.

name: Café Cutie Pie
review: It is very good that they have not just a changing table but also a play area for the kids. As a mother, you feel very welcomed to drink your coffee :)

name: Tierpark Berlin (Schlossseite)
review: It's on the side of the Schloss at the Tierpark and in good condition. You can enter with a stroller and it's not crowded.

name: Europa Center
review: Located in 1EG , in the restroom area close to Saturn. It costs 0.5 but has lots of space and it is pretty clean, the changing mat was nice and comfortable also.

name: FEZ, Café Zauberbaum
review: Changing table in men‘s AND women’s restroom!! Enough space, but a Dyson hand dryer nearby (a little bit noisy for our little ones)

name: Il Ritrovo
review: It seems dirty and messy, but actually it is very clean and there is lots of space for a baby, a stroller, to leave your stuff while changing etc. Very good.

name: Sana Klinikum Lichtenberg Sozialpädiatrisches Zentrum
review: Very good and clean. It was not a busy day when we went there, so not sure how it would be if the emergency room would be full…

name: Vivantes Klinikum Am Urban
review: The table itself is fine but the hospital emergency is not a nice place…

name: porta Möbel
review: Lots of space for a stroller inside the room. Changing table is simple, but very good.

name: EDEKA Treugut
review: You must ask for the key in the bakery.

name: Gasthaus Figl
review: It is a foldable changing table „forgot“ in the corner, but pretty ok to use.

name: Smart Kids - Family Play Café
review: People need to take their shoes off. It is a shoes-free-space.

name: T.Viet
review: It's not a diaper changing table, but a dresser with space for it on top.

name: dm
review: It is located on the second floor of the store.

name: Kurhaus Zingst
review:
You have to ask at the front desk to open the door to the wheelchair toilet.
Best and cleanest changing table for me so far!

name: dm
review: Inside Markthalle Neun

name: Rastanlage Frankenwald West
review: Very good! You can use it for free. There is 2 changing tables in a dedicated room and another one in the wheelchair toilett. Plenty space for a stroller.

name: Schnitzelei Charlottenburg
review: Nice restaurant to go with kids, they give them something to paint and have a decent changing table. Just be careful going down the tricky stairs holding a baby!

name: Shopping Via Catarina
review: One of the best places I have ever seen to change diapers. They have many changing tables (female toilet, dedicaded room etc) and they also have mini toilets for toddlers in both female and male toilets. Amazing!

name: Publix
review: Lots of space, very clean, with elevator to access the lower level with a stroller. Pretty good and recommended — also because you can sneak in and use the changing table without having to buy anything at the café.

name: dm
review: Almost all DM stores have a changing table and some have a nursing area too :)

name: Parthenon
review:
It's not for short people, but worked for me. 
When you use it the bathroom is blocked.

name: Trinity Porto
address: R. dos Heróis e dos Mártires de Angola 49, 4000-285 Porto, Portugal
review: There is a mini toilet for troddlers and a chair for breastfeeding.

name: LA MAISON
review: Very dirty. Not enough light (very dark toilet to perform a diaper change).

name: Ristorante Pavarotti
review: You have to go downstairs to access the toilet.

name: Ludothek global (Global Village)
review: It‘s clean and you have plenty space.

name: Dussmann
review:
The changing table is right next to the sinks, where very loud hand dryers go off every couple of seconds (when it's busy) and that scared the hell out of my baby and she cried terribly! would never change diapers there again while busy. 

No photo because of the streeeeeess

name: Berliner Toilette Weichselstraße
review: It was out of order when I passed by on July 18, 2025.

name: ZOLA
review: There isn't a diaper changing station as such, but there are several sofas and tables on the first floor and in the basement (where the toilets are) that could be used for this purpose. I have changed a diaper there.

name: KALLE HALLE Markthalle
review: Room for stroller and table is clean. The lighting could be a little better.

name: dm
review: Mega complete. They have all supplies that are needed to change a diaper :)

name: Zum Fliegenden Holländer
review: The changing table is a desk in the hallway that leads to the bathrooms. Both the bathrooms and the table are upstairs

name: Ostsee Park
review: There are three changing tables located in small separate areas in the women's and men's restrooms (yes!). There is also a changing table in the accessible restroom. Amazing!

name: Fridas Eiskrem
review: You need to go down to the toilets so no chance to get there with stroller, but it’s outside the women’s and men’s restrooms so everybody can change diapers ;)

name: Boulevard Laçador
review: Very good one!

name: Standesamt Neukölln
review: Not very beautiful, but you can always use on an emergency.

name: dm
review: very good condition, at the diapers section

name: Rossmann
review: Located in the second floor (with elevator) in the kids section of the store.

name: ALEX
review: Very low.

name: Serways Raststätte Schafstrift Nord
review: Amazing and clean. You have a dedicated room with a sink and a small seat. Enough space to move and pull stuff out of the bag. Paper to use as mat. Top!

name: Viva Open Mall
review: Very nice family bathroom where you can chance a diaper or breastfeed. You can also use the toilet.

name: Aeroporto Sá Carneiro
address: Pedras Rubras, 4470-558 Vila Nova da Telha, Portugal

name: Autohof Thiersheim
review:
Absolut gem on the A93.
They have a Restaurant with a big play area for kids all ages and this not super nice looking, but very well equipped changing table! Much recommended!

name: Cais Embarcadero
review: It is inside a container (like all the toilets there), but pretty good and clean.

name: Arlberg WellCom
review: There is a family changing room with changing table available.

name: Daryas Spielcafé
review: The changing table is mounted inside the restroom. It's foldable. There is also a teddy-bear-shaped toilet seat insert for toddlers and a small stool so little ones can reach the sink to wash their hands.

name: Öffentliche Toilette
review: Kostenlos

name: The Boat Kaffee
review: Cramped space, but at least they have one.

name: Tierra Garat Porfirio
address: Av Porfirio Díaz 66, Noche Buena, Benito Juárez, 03720

name: Ikea
review: Accessibility is good, suitable for both parents. There's a chair for breastfeeding. It's constantly cleaned. The downside is its proximity to the dining area and the high volume of people passing by; despite having a door, there's a lot of noise.

name: 60 seconds to napoli
review: Unfortunately dirty (needs to be washed)

name: RumBa
review: It’s a family café, it looks dusty and dirty from the outside but is really clean and the people are so nice and friendly

name: Zauberküche
review: Really beautiful place for brunch, but the changing room is full with other stuff too so space is limited

name: Forum Gesundheit Neukölln e.V.
review: I have changed my baby many times there during my postpartum course. It is very nice that they have it. The space is a bit tight, but it is doable.

name: Sphere Bar im Fernsehturm
review: It was a toilet and was adjusted to a diaper station. It’s tight and not so comfy but at least you have a place to change hehe

name: Obi Lichtenberg
review: The changing table was fine, but the room didnt smell too good

name: Zenner
review: Update from 2/10 to 4/10 😅 the cover is better, but it's still not cleaned every day (yesterday's dirt is still there today 🙊). Table is still located next to the Dyson hand dryer.

name: PalaisPopulaire
review: Very clean, you don’t have to go in the museum

name: Aeroporto Internacional de Porto Alegre - Salgado Filho
review: It is located on the toilet door. No privacy at all — but maybe there are better ones located in another floor at the airport (this one is next to the food court, second floor).

name: Gropius Bau
review: Not sure if I like changing my baby in the middle of the female restroom. It would be better a discret corner or its own room. Plus point because they have small toilets for kids.

name: InterContinental Porto - Palácio das Cardosas
review: It is in the hotel and if you ask nicely they probably will let you use it.

name: Tapiocaria
review: Decent and clean, but very cramped and installed too high. Bad for short parents

name: Serways Raststätte Aichen Nord
review: Mega dirty!

name: FaNN - Familienhaus Neukölln Nord
review: They have 2 (each one in one bathroom). They are both very practical, they offer diapers.

name: Ishin Mittelstraße
review: It's pretty simple, but it also matches the restaurant itself. Bonus point: they are SUPER kids friendly.

name: Erchy‘s
review: Close to the urinol, but the table is very good. Is a foldable one so if you’re baby moves a lot, it might be not so ideal.

name: MEDICOLEO
review: Nice that they have one

name: Boulevard Berlin
review: Wärmelampe

name: Cafe Babette
review: Very good one! Only problem is that you can just access the cafeteria floor by stairs (just one part of the cafe has access via lift). Cappuccino is 2,80€!!!

name: La Maison des Histoires
review: Small toilets for kids.

name: Familienzentrum Juli
review: Super nice, lots of space, always clean.

name: Al Theatro
address: Campo S. Fantin, 1916, 30124 Venezia VE, Italy
review: Good space, looks comfy, it's in the wheelchair bathroom so men and women can enter.

name: Kaffeehaus Madlen
review: Really clean and looks like new, it’s also super cute. There are wickel unterlagen available.

name: Forum Schwanthalerhöhe
review: There is a big Family Toilet with changing table, also in the regular womens toilets there is a big stall where you can go inside with a stroller, which is very convenient!

name: Kiez & Kugel
review: The changing table is merely a normal table in the bathroom but it is sufficient

name: Gal Café
address: Rua do Forno do Tijolo 54A 1170-172 Lisboa
review: The bathroom is small, but they still found a way to install a changing table.

name: Camponesa
address: Rod. Pres. Castello Branco, s/n, km 198, Zona Rural, 18640-000

name: Umami
review: There is plenty space in the toilet and it looks clean. The restaurant is super kids-friendly. They are SUPER nice to kids (and parents with baby/kids).

name: Kadewe
review: It is located on the 7th floor. Accessible by elevator. Separate from the restrooms, with a tap available and a changing mat.

name: Bourbon Shopping
review: They have a space for families, a family toilet, with changing table and a toilet for the minis. Amazing!

name: A Padaria Portuguesa
review: Not so much space. In front of the sink. If you use it, other people can‘t access the toilet. No easy access (stair).

name: Zooparque
address: Avenida ZooParque Itatiba, Rodovia Dom Pedro I, KM 95.5, 13254-762

name: Havab
review: Very nice and clean.

name: Serways Raststätte Fränkische Schweiz-Pegnitz West
review: They have a heat lamp, paper to cover the changing mat and a special (closed) trash for diapers. Also a chair to lock your toddler if you want to use the toilet. Excelent!

name: Haus der Wannsee Konferenz
review: Never expected a changing table there in the museum but it was in a good but a little bit dirty condition

name: The Playce
review: It is located in OG level of the mall near the restaurant zone. You pay for your use 0.7 euro (you can change the diaper, then return to the women's restroom with no extra cost). it is a nice dedicated room, it has a chair that could be used for breastfeeding as well, clean and spacious.

name: Pingo Doce Boavista
review: Terrible. Floor mega dirty. No toilet paper. I suggest to avoid it, but if you have no other option, then give it a try — maybe you have more luck than me.

name: Amoreiras Shopping
review: Accessible bathroom, between Zara and the Jean Loius David hairdressing salon. It's on the second floor. There are small individual toilets with a changing table and a toilet available for children too. A spacious area that can be accessed with a stroller. Very convenient for moms and dads.

name: monkey bar | neni Berlin
review: it's a bit dark, but a good changing table in general!

name: Das Café in der Gartenakademie
review: A small table but conditions are okay, it’s left side of the women’s toilet

name: dm
review: inside the East Side Mall

name: Baby & Beans
review:
This is a coffee shop with a playground area full of Montessori and sensory toys, very safe, fun and friendly and with baby food (no salt, no sugar, no gluten).
Owned by two mothers.

name: Gasthaus Seibl Fam. Bayer
review: The changing table is good but the restaurant service is TERRIBLE. I don‘t recommend it.

name: La Fève
review: The table is okay, but the cafe is mega kids friendly! They have a kids corner and you should leave your stroller outside the place — but they lock it up themselves outside :))

name: dm
review: There is also a "Stilecke" available :)

name: Le Brot
review: It is in a very small room and a bit improvised — but at least they have one! Extra points because they have a kids corner for the small ones :)

name: Kaffee Barista
review: It's downstairs, so no stroller, but clean and enough space

name: Tomasa
review: Very confortable and clean, but need to take stairs to access it.

name: Wolf Kino
review: Very good! It is also a must since they offer a session for babies on tuesday morning :)

name: KERB Berlin
review: The room/toilet could be better lit.

name: McDonald's
review: Very good option for a change while travelling! Our stroller is small and did not fit in the room. There is even a warm lamp (see photo).

name: L'Oceanogrŕfic
address: C/ d'Eduardo Primo Yúfera, 1, Quatre Carreres, 46013

name: Spore Initiative
review: Very good one!

name: Hotel Zoe
review: The changing table is metal and I wasnt very sure if it would hold my baby. All well, but didnt have trust at first
